title: Singapore Eyes Hedge Fund Tax Cuts to Boost Hub Role: FT
headline_summary: Singapore eyes hedge fund tax cuts to boost hub role
blurb: Singapore's Monetary Authority is negotiating tax cuts with investment firms to reduce the tax burden on hedge fund managers. The move targets talent retention and regional competitiveness as firms migrate across Asia and beyond. For LPs and fund operators, this signals a critical lever in the financial hub wars—tax policy now competes with regulatory efficiency and deal flow access.

title: Tom Blomfield is taking a leave of absence from YC to join Anthropic
headline_summary: Tom Blomfield leaves Y Combinator to join Anthropic
blurb: Tom Blomfield is taking leave from Y Combinator to join Anthropic. Blomfield founded Wise and spent years at YC; he's now moving to the AI company as it scales toward becoming a core player in frontier AI infrastructure. The move reflects how top venture talent is consolidating around AI's highest-stakes bets, clearly signaling where the best builders see capital flowing next.

title: OpenAI power consolidates under co-founder Greg Brockman ahead of prospective IPO
headline_summary: Greg Brockman consolidates OpenAI power as Simo departs
blurb: Greg Brockman consolidates operational authority at OpenAI as Fidji Simo departs due to a chronic medical condition. Simo held a senior product role; his exit reshuffles leadership ahead of OpenAI's expected IPO. C-suite reshuffles at pre-IPO unicorns typically precede going-public processes. This signals governance restructuring and investor positioning: the company and backers believe the executive team can handle public-market scrutiny.

title: EDX Markets Closes $76M Series C Led by SBI Holdings
headline_summary: EDX Markets closes $76M Series C led by SBI Holdings
blurb: EDX Markets, an institutional-only crypto trading venue with its own central clearinghouse, closed a $76 million Series C funding round. SBI Holdings, the Tokyo-listed financial group, led the round and became a strategic investor. The funding signals strong institutional appetite for regulated crypto infrastructure and reflects growing mainstream finance acceptance of professional-grade digital asset trading as a core business capability.

title: Corgi, a startup that insures other startups, doubled its valuation to $2.6 billion three weeks after its last round — and because the same investors backed both, the jump is drawing quiet scrutiny over “internal markups”
headline_summary: Corgi doubles valuation to $2.6B in three weeks, sparking LP concerns
blurb: Corgi, a startup insurance platform, doubled its valuation to $2.6 billion—just three weeks after the same investors led its previous round. The rapid markup is drawing quiet scrutiny from limited partners, who question whether the increase reflects actual revenue growth or represents inflated paper valuations designed to boost portfolio returns. The tension signals a broader LP concern about how much weight internal markups should carry in fund performance metrics.

title: Nebius raised $775 million by borrowing against its GPUs. It has $40 billion more contracts to securitise.
headline_summary: Nebius securitizes GPU infrastructure: $775M raised, $40B contract pipeline
blurb: Nebius raised $775 million in secured debt backed by GPU infrastructure and customer contracts from an investment-grade buyer. The facility carries SOFR+250 basis points (roughly 6.8% currently) and matures in October 2030, with collateral over-securing the loan. The company can securitize an additional $40 billion in contracts, signaling that debt markets now treat AI infrastructure as reliable, bankable collateral.

title: AI Chip Startup Etched Is in Talks for $20 Billion Valuation
headline_summary: Etched AI chip startup talks $20B valuation, raises at $10B
blurb: Etched, an AI chip startup founded by Harvard dropouts, is negotiating a $20 billion valuation while simultaneously raising capital at a $10 billion valuation in a Sequoia-led round. The dual-track funding signals intense investor appetite for proprietary AI chip design. Semis remain the core infrastructure play in AI; whoever controls the silicon controls downstream compute costs and supply chain leverage.

title: Databricks raising new funding at $188B valuation
blurb: Databricks closed a $3 billion funding round at a $188 billion valuation, with Coatue Management leading the investment. The company is a data infrastructure and machine learning platform serving enterprise customers globally, now ranking among the world's most valuable private technology companies. For VCs, the mega-round demonstrates sustained investor appetite for AI and data tooling despite significant macroeconomic headwinds across broader software markets.

title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI’s favorite second act
headline_summary: Databricks hits $188B valuation, solidifying AI infrastructure leadership
blurb: Databricks hit $188B valuation in its latest funding round, cementing its position as the market's primary AI infrastructure bet. The data platform has fully repositioned itself into AI and ML, publishing research demonstrating significant cost savings from open-weight models for coding tasks. The milestone signals sustained institutional appetite for infrastructure enabling faster, cheaper AI development and deployment at enterprise scale.

title: Nuclear startup Valar Atomics in talks to raise new funding at $6B valuation
headline_summary: Valar Atomics raises at $6B, highlights complex funding trend
blurb: Nuclear startup Valar Atomics is in talks to raise capital at a $6B valuation. The round exemplifies a growing trend where complex, multi-stage funding structures obscure true entry prices and per-share costs for investors. For VCs and LPs, headline valuations now routinely mask real economics—diving into deal architecture is essential to understand actual ownership stakes, pricing mechanics, and expected returns on capital.

title: Nvidia-backed Fireworks hits $17.5 billion valuation as companies pursue cheaper AI models
headline_summary: Fireworks hits $17.5B: cheaper AI inference gaining enterprise traction
blurb: Fireworks, an AI inference startup backed by Nvidia, reached a $17.5 billion valuation. The company previously depended heavily on Cursor for most of its revenue but has now diversified as enterprises seek cheaper AI model hosting and inference options. The valuation demonstrates strong market demand for cost-efficient AI infrastructure separate from large foundation model builders and established cloud giants.

title: Lakestar closes €262.2 million Resilience I Fund to back European defence and dual-use companies
headline_summary: Lakestar closes €262M fund for European defence and dual-use tech
blurb: Lakestar closed Resilience I at €262.2 million, heavily oversubscribed with investor demand significantly exceeding the target raise. The fund is Europe's largest institutional venture vehicle built exclusively for dual-use and defence-tech companies, funded entirely from private capital. The close signals material LP appetite for geopolitical-strategic hard-tech—a clear pivot away from consumer/SaaS that reflects growing institutional confidence in European defence innovation.

title: Moonshot plans to list in Hong Kong within six months at a $30 billion valuation
headline_summary: Moonshot plans $30B Hong Kong IPO within six months
blurb: Moonshot AI is pursuing a Hong Kong IPO within six months at a $30 billion-plus valuation. The three-year-old AI company has distributed shareholder resolutions supporting the public listing and is simultaneously finalizing a major fundraising round. The planned exit demonstrates genuine investor appetite for AI infrastructure companies, even as the broader market cools on inflated late-stage valuations and deal activity across Asia.

title: Sources: Kimi developer Moonshot told investors it is preparing for a Hong Kong IPO in as early as six months; its ARR hit $300M in June, up from $200M in April (Bloomberg)
headline_summary: Moonshot AI eyes Hong Kong IPO within six months, $300M ARR
blurb: Moonshot AI, the developer of the frontier AI assistant Kimi, is preparing a Hong Kong IPO within six months. The startup hit $300M ARR in June, up 50% from $200M in April. This signals frontier AI startups are accessing public markets faster than traditional software—shortening the private funding window and indicating strong appetite among IPO investors for AI exits.

title: Moonshot Plans IPO in Six Months After China AI Breakthrough
headline_summary: Moonshot AI eyes IPO in six months after model breakthrough
blurb: Moonshot AI will list within six months according to investor communications. The Chinese AI startup's latest model has shifted perceptions of China's AI capabilities and moved tech stocks lower. The IPO reflects investor confidence in frontier AI builders and reveals sustained appetite for AI commercialization amid geopolitical tensions and increasing regulatory scrutiny of Chinese AI competitors and their market position.

title: How Apple’s big lawsuit could disrupt OpenAI’s IPO plans
headline_summary: Apple trade secrets lawsuit threatens OpenAI's IPO plans
blurb: Apple filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI, alleging misconduct involving the chief hardware officer and claiming 400+ former Apple employees were hired. OpenAI's response has been carefully hedged, offering limited clarity. The lawsuit's timing is devastating: it directly threatens OpenAI's reported IPO plans, signaling material exit delays and regulatory risk for the AI sector.

title: Germany’s Prior Labs raises €1 billion and exits to SAP 18 months after being founded
headline_summary: Prior Labs exits to SAP for €1 billion, 18 months after founding
blurb: Prior Labs, a Berlin-based frontier AI lab founded just 18 months ago, has been acquired by SAP. The enterprise software giant invested €1 billion to fund infrastructure, hiring, and long-term frontier research, with Prior Labs retaining its independent brand and research agenda. The rapid exit signals strong enterprise demand for frontier AI talent and R&D firepower, reinforcing SAP's strategic pivot toward AI-driven enterprise software.

title: Anthropic moves closer to mega-IPO as bankers line up investor meetings
headline_summary: Anthropic lines up investor meetings for potential October IPO
blurb: Anthropic is preparing for an IPO targeting October 2026, lining up investor meetings for a mega-offering. The AI startup behind Claude is racing to beat OpenAI and become the first major generative AI company to go public. The move signals strong institutional appetite for large-scale AI infrastructure, marking a major liquidity milestone for Anthropic's backers and the broader AI industry.
